positive.description: An interface and factory hide a single concrete call path.
positive.patch:
+type runner interface { Run() error }
+func newRunner() runner { return &onlyRunner{} }

negative.description: A short direct helper replaces duplicated control flow.

applicability.description: A constant value changes without changing concepts or control flow.

crossLens.description: A missing authentication check belongs to the security lens.

positive.description: A wire length is trusted before allocation.

negative.description: A decoder rejects an oversized frame before allocation.
negative.patch:
+if n > maxFrame { return ErrFrameTooLarge }
+buf := make([]byte, n)

applicability.description: An internal arithmetic helper does not cross an input boundary.
applicability.patch: +func add(a, b int) int { return a + b }

crossLens.description: An unbounded request queue belongs to overload control.

positive.description: An exported method is removed without a compatibility path.

negative.description: A deprecated exported method remains as a forwarding shim.
negative.patch:
+// LegacyCall is deprecated: use Call.
+func (c *Client) LegacyCall() error { return c.Call() }

applicability.description: A private local variable is renamed.

crossLens.description: A race in private cache state belongs to local concurrency.

positive.description: A reusable package reads application-global configuration.

negative.description: The caller supplies package configuration explicitly.

applicability.description: A leaf function fixes an off-by-one error without changing a seam.

crossLens.description: A parser allocation from an untrusted length belongs to input integrity.

positive.description: A third sibling repeats the same stateful lifecycle mechanism.

negative.description: Two similar-looking functions encode deliberately different domain rules.
negative.patch:
+func debitLimit(a Account) int { return a.DebitCap }
+func creditLimit(a Account) int { return a.CreditCap }

applicability.description: Only one concrete mechanism exists in the repository evidence.

crossLens.description: A single speculative interface belongs to the simplicity lens.

positive.description: A background goroutine has no cancellation or join path.

negative.description: The component accepts cancellation and exposes a completion signal.

applicability.description: A pure value transformation creates no runtime resource.

crossLens.description: An exported wire shape change belongs to contract evolution.
crossLens.patch:
-type Reply struct { ID string }
+type Reply struct { Key string }

positive.description: A retried remote mutation has no idempotency key.

negative.description: The caller supplies a stable operation ID across retries.
negative.patch: +for attempt := 0; attempt < 3; attempt++ { err = client.Charge(opID, amount) }

applicability.description: A process-local slice operation crosses no distributed boundary.

crossLens.description: A local unsynchronized map belongs to the local concurrency lens.

positive.description: Caller-controlled work enters an unbounded in-memory queue.

negative.description: A bounded channel rejects work with an explicit full policy.
negative.patch: +select { case queue <- job: return nil; default: return ErrBusy }

applicability.description: A fixed-size local array is independent of caller load.

crossLens.description: A cross-node retry without idempotency belongs to distributed invariants.

positive.description: A performance claim replaces clear code without a benchmark baseline.
positive.patch:
+// Faster than encoding/json.
+func encodeFast(v Value) []byte { return unsafeEncode(v) }

negative.description: The patch includes before/after benchmark and allocation evidence.
negative.patch: +// benchstat: -18.2% ns/op, -1 alloc/op across representative inputs.

advisory.description: Correctness validation adds a measurable pass to benchmark-covered decoding without making a speed claim.
advisory.patch: +for _, register := range regs { if register > maxRho { return ErrorInvalidData } }

applicability.description: A correctness fix makes no performance claim and has no supplied profile.
applicability.patch:
-if err != nil { return nil }
+if err != nil { return err }

crossLens.description: A panic on malformed input belongs to input integrity.

positive.description: User input is concatenated into a shell command.

negative.description: Arguments are passed directly without a shell interpreter.

applicability.description: A private numeric helper handles no untrusted data or privilege.

crossLens.description: An internal package dependency leak belongs to package boundaries.

positive.description: A remote request has no trace or metric correlation.

negative.description: A request records bounded latency and propagates trace context.

applicability.description: A pure parser helper does not alter production diagnosis paths.

crossLens.description: A goroutine leak belongs to runtime lifecycle.

positive.description: Two goroutines write a map without synchronization.

negative.description: All map access is protected by the same mutex.

applicability.description: Sequential code has no goroutine, shared mutable state, channel, or lock.

crossLens.description: A retry across a remote boundary belongs to distributed invariants.
